canonical_name: JANUS A18.43 V0.2 — Through the Horizon
purpose: Couple TimeShift freeze and Undina Native Admission Gate as two interlocked airlock hatches so control ordering is explicit, acknowledged, idempotent and fail-closed.
inspiration_note: The name is inspired by the airlock/decompression imagery of the film Event Horizon and is recorded as a respectful cultural reference, not an endorsement or affiliation.
canonical_principle: Asynchronous work inside each phase; a strict rendezvous barrier between phases.

airlock_mapping.freeze_seal: TimeShift whole-process FREEZE with quiescence proof
airlock_mapping.repressurization: REOPEN exactly one new admission epoch

hard_interlocks: no new admissions during controlled decompression
no REOPEN before two stable zero observations
no stale control_epoch or wave_epoch acknowledgement
no PID, session or reconnect change across a boundary
duplicate action_id is idempotent; conflicting reuse is rejected

order: abort and thaw immediately if the latest-safe horizon was crossed
two_phase_timeshift.reason: Eliminate snapshot-to-suspend lead debt by deciding from a motionless state.

controlled_decompression_invariants: admitted_total remains exactly equal to gate-close baseline
old epoch remains active until stable vacuum
all old completions are foam/drain, never a new crest
fault_policy: Any missing acknowledgement, identity drift, reconnect delta, new admission during drain, queue/in-flight increase or event-chain failure moves the coordinator to FAIL_CLOSED.
prohibited_claims: energy saving before matched live telemetry
hardware lifetime extension from a short run
